audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra offering instruction text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for reparation for fraud/theft, restitution plus fifth, priestly instructions for burnt offering, grain offering, priestly grain offering, and purification offering.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
When a soul/life sins/misaligns and acts unfaithfully against YHWH, deceiving his companion concerning deposit, pledge, robbery, or by oppressing his companion,
or has found lost thing and lies concerning it, and swears falsely concerning one from all that a human does to sin/misalign in them,
then it shall be, because he sinned/misaligned and became guilty, he shall return the robbed thing he robbed, or oppression he oppressed, or deposit deposited with him, or lost thing he found,
or anything concerning which he swore falsely; he shall repay it by its head and add its fifth to it; to the one to whom it belongs he shall give it on day of his guilt/reparation.
His guilt/reparation offering he shall bring to YHWH: a complete ram from flock, by your valuation, for guilt/reparation offering to priest.
The priest shall make atonement/covering for him before YHWH, and it shall be forgiven him for any of all he did to become guilty by it.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

Command Aaron and his sons, saying: This is instruction/Torah of burnt offering. Burnt offering itself shall remain on hearth upon altar all night until morning, and fire of altar shall be kept burning on it.
The priest shall put on his linen garment, with linen undergarments on his flesh; he shall lift ashes to which fire consumed burnt offering on altar and put them beside altar.
He shall strip off his garments and put on other garments, and bring ashes outside camp to clean place.

Fire on altar shall be kept burning on it; it shall not go out. Priest shall burn wood on it morning by morning, arrange burnt offering on it, and turn fat of peace/well-being offerings into smoke on it.
Fire continually shall be kept burning on altar; it shall not go out.
This is instruction/Torah of grain offering: sons of Aaron shall bring it near before YHWH, before altar.
One shall lift from it handful of fine flour of grain offering and from its oil and all frankincense on grain offering, and turn into smoke on altar as soothing scent, its memorial portion to YHWH.
What remains from it Aaron and his sons shall eat; unleavened it shall be eaten in holy place; in court of tent of meeting they shall eat it.
It shall not be baked leavened. I have given it as their portion from my fire-offerings. Holy of Holies it is, like sin/misalignment offering and guilt/reparation offering.
Every male among children of Aaron may eat it, age-long due throughout your generations from fire-offerings of YHWH. Everything that touches them shall become holy.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
This is offering of Aaron and his sons that they shall bring near to YHWH on day he is anointed: tenth ephah of fine flour as regular grain offering, half in morning and half in evening.
On griddle with oil it shall be made; well mixed you shall bring it; baked pieces of grain offering you shall bring near as soothing scent to YHWH.
The priest anointed in his place from among his sons shall do it; age-long statute to YHWH. Whole offering it shall be turned into smoke.
Every grain offering of priest shall be whole offering; it shall not be eaten.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying: This is instruction/Torah of sin/misalignment offering. In place where burnt offering is slaughtered, sin/misalignment offering shall be slaughtered before YHWH. Holy of Holies it is.
The priest who offers it for sin/misalignment shall eat it; in holy place it shall be eaten, in court of tent of meeting.
Everything touching its flesh shall become holy; and when any of its blood/life is spattered on garment, what it spattered on you shall wash in holy place.
Earthen vessel in which it is boiled shall be broken; if boiled in bronze vessel, it shall be scoured and rinsed with water.
Every male among priests may eat it; Holy of Holies it is.
But any sin/misalignment offering from whose blood/life is brought into tent of meeting to make atonement/covering in holy place shall not be eaten; in fire it shall be burned.

translation_notes: Fraud against neighbor is also unfaithfulness against YHWH; repair requires restitution plus fifth before/with ritual offering.
Ash handling and garments show ordinary cleanup is part of holy service.
Continual altar fire is tended; holiness includes maintenance, not just dramatic ritual.
Priestly portions are provision, but priestly grain offering is wholly offered.
Holy contagion language here is ritual-state language, not moral disgust.
safety_note: Leviticus 6 insists that spiritual repair does not replace material restitution. Stolen, withheld, or fraudulently held goods must be returned with added repair.
